2026-04-10.patch_summary: This patch introduces a per-page_counter stock to the Linux kernel's memory management system, allowing each page counter to have its own cache of pre-charged pages and reducing the need for expensive hierarchy walks on every memcg charge and limit check. This addresses two main issues with the current implementation: the random eviction of memcgs when more than 7 are being charged, and the tight coupling between stock and memcg, which prevents new page counters from using separate stock caches. The patch adds a generic per-cpu stock to the struct page_counter and provides functions for enabling and disabling stock on a per-page_counter basis.
